name: SATAN2

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo apt install gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64   # Debian/Ubuntu`. Further invocations from upstream: `cargo check --workspace`; `cargo build --release -p satan2-cli`. ☢️ SATAN2 Secure Anti-Forensics and Total Annihilation of iNformation.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: ☢️ SATAN2 Secure Anti-Forensics and Total Annihilation of iNformation.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: ☢️ SATAN2 Secure Anti-Forensics and Total Annihilation of iNformation.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use SATAN2 because ☢️ SATAN2 Secure Anti-Forensics and Total Annihilation of iNformation.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo apt install gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64   # Debian/Ubuntu` as documented upstream for SATAN2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cargo check --workspace` as documented upstream for SATAN2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cargo build --release -p satan2-cli` as documented upstream for SATAN2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cargo build --release -p satan2-win --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu` as documented upstream for SATAN2.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair SATAN2 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `SATAN2 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: ata-erase <DEV>                 ATA Secure Erase (requires unfrozen drive)
nvme-erase <DEV>                NVMe Format NVM (Crypto Erase when available)
